dhp21:1:
Heedfulness:
the path to the Deathless.
dhp21:4: The heedless are as if already dead.
dhp22:1: Knowing this as a true distinction,
dhp22:4: enjoying the range of the noble ones.
dhp23:1: The enlightened, constantly absorbed in jhana,
dhp23:4: the unexcelled rest from the yoke.
dhp24:2: clean in action, acting with due consideration,
dhp25:3: the wise would make an island
dhp26:3: while one who is wise cherishes heedfulness
dhp27:2: or to intimacy with sensual delight–
dhp27:3: for a heedful person, absorbed in jhana,
dhp28:1: When the wise person drives out heedlessness
dhp28:3: having climbed the high tower of discernment,
dhp28:4: sorrow-free, he observes the sorrowing crowd–
dhp28:5: as the enlightened man, having scaled a summit,
dhp28:6: the fools on the ground below.
dhp29:3: just as a fast horse advances, leaving the weak behind:
dhp32:4: stands right on the verge of Unbinding.
